explanation: This command simulates a benign use of ConfigSecurityPolicy.exe with a URL parameter that matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It uses a standard HTTP URL to trigger the rule while remaining safe for testing.
This command uses the HTTPS protocol instead of HTTP, demonstrating a safe mutation via substitution of the URL scheme while still matching the Sigma rule's detection logic.
This command uses the FTP protocol and long-form option syntax to demonstrate reordering and substitution. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses an alternative syntax (key=value) to pass the URL parameter, demonstrating safe mutation via recoding. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection logic.
This command omits the explicit parameter name and directly passes the URL as an argument. It demonstrates omission while still matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
